page: OVHcloud b3-128 is a general-purpose server powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The hardware profile includes 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD for local storage. Network connectivity is supported by an 8 Gbps baseline bandwidth, and the instance runs on a KVM hypervisor.

Benchmark evaluations place the b3-128 in the top tier for stress-ng single-core CPU performance, while multi-core CPU performance and Geekbench scores rank in the strong top 25% category. Memory bandwidth tests, including both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads and writes, consistently register in the top 25%. In application-specific workloads, the server demonstrates strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations, alongside a high throughput of 74.67 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks.

Given its dedicated core allocation and balanced resource density, the b3-128 offers a highly stable performance profile without the resource contention typical of shared-core environments, representing a cost-efficient option for sustained workloads. The combination of 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of RAM, and fast local NVMe storage makes this server well-suited for relational databases, Redis caching layers, software compilation pipelines, and general-purpose web serving applications.
description: The OVHcloud b3-128 is a general-purpose server built on the KVM hypervisor and powered by an AMD EPYC Milan processor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores running at 2.3 GHz with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, eliminating hyperthreading overhead. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD, and an 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, placing it in the top 25% for memory bandwidth, software compilation, and database operations. With its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated hardware allocation, the b3-128 provides a stable and cost-efficient environment for workloads such as relational databases, caching layers, and CI/CD compilation pipelines.
og_description: OVHcloud b3-128 is a general-purpose server featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 128 GB of RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD, delivering strong database and multi-core CPU performance.
meta_description: OVHcloud b3-128 is a general-purpose server with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan cores, 128 GB RAM, and a 400 GB NVMe SSD on a KVM hypervisor.
tagline: A dedicated thirty-two core AMD EPYC server with balanced memory and fast NVMe storage for database and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC Milan physical cores running at 2.3 GHz
128 GB of RAM paired with a bundled 400 GB NVMe SSD
Top-tier single-core and strong multi-core CPU benchmark performance
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Optimized for relational databases, caching, and software compilation
